question_text: A sharp knife blade has an area of 0.002 m² in contact with a tomato. A force of 50 N is applied to cut the tomato. Calculate the pressure exerted by the knife blade on the tomato.
explanation: Using the formula P = F/A, we divide the force (50 N) by the area (0.002 m²) to get 25000 Pa. The small contact area of the sharp blade creates a very high pressure, which is why sharp knives cut easily.
